↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Wreckage from the USAF aircrafts found this morning near Isfahan indicates that at least 2 MH-6 Little Bird helicopters were destroyed there. Two large wrecks confirms the previous claim of two destroyed C-130. What is interesting is that the crash site is beyond the maximum stated range of the small helicopter. The MH-6 may have landed at the temporary airfield to refuel from a C-130. The Little Bird can't refuel in the air. It is possible that the C-130 were unable to refuel them after sustaining damage. Out of fuel and unable to reach friendly territory, the MH-6 were destroyed there. 🤔 Hopefully Hollywood will make us a movie out of it and shed the light on the mystery. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel Trump: We have rescued the seriously wounded, and really brave, F-15 Crew Member/Officer, from deep inside the mountains of Iran. The Iranian Military was looking hard, in big numbers, and getting close. He is a highly respected Colonel. This type of raid is seldom attempted because of the danger to “man and equipment.” It just doesn’t happen! The second raid came after the first one, where we rescued the pilot in broad daylight, also unusual, spending seven hours over Iran. An AMAZING show of bravery and talent by all! I will be having a News Conference, with the Military, at the Oval Office, on Monday, at 1:00 P.M. God Bless our great MILITARY WARRIORS! President DONALD J. TRUMP tweet

🇵🇸 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 Gaza Ministry of Health: – "A total of 4 martyrs (1 newly reported, 3 recovered) and 5 injuries arrived at hospitals in the Gaza Strip over the past 24 hours." – "Since the ceasefire (11 October): • Total martyrs: 716 • Total injuries: 1,968 • Total recovered bodies: 759"

U.S. used 2 × C-130s + 4 × MH-6 “Little Bird” helicopters to rescuse F-15E crew member from Iran. Pilot was rescued from mountain area by helicopter. Problem: C-130s got stuck on a makeshift desert airstrip. Additional aircraft (Dash-8s) brought in to extract personnel. Then, U.S. abandoned and destroyed aircraft (~$300 million loss). Iran shot down 2 MQ-9 Reaper drones. U.S. struck approaching IRGC forces. Casualties: None reported on U.S. side. Source: michaeldweiss (at X)

🐻 As for the rescue operation… we’ll analyze it later. There are so many conflicting stories and wild speculation floating around that it’s getting ridiculous. Our quick take: they got the pilots out—but it was a total mess. One thing worth noting: until the U.S. releases some kind of evidence (even something as basic as a blurred photo of the crew), people have every right to question what actually happened. Look at Nicolás Maduro—when he was captured, images flooded out almost immediately. In this case, all we have are statements from U.S. officials relayed through U.S. media, plus photos of burnt-out aircraft. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 IRGC said its Naval and Aerospace forces carried out a joint "retaliatory operation" targeting energy infrastructure and US-linked facilities across the region, as part of Wave 96 of Operation True Promise 4. In a statement, the IRGC detailed that the operation followed US-Israeli attacks on the B1 bridge in Karaj and petrochemical industries in Mahshahr, adding that "the first phase of the response… was carried out by setting fire to Zionist targets in the occupied territories and US economic interests in regional countries." The first phase of wave 96 targeted: 1. A refinery supplying fuel to jets in Haifa 2. US gas facilities of Exxon Mobil and Chevron in Habshan, the UAE 3. US petrochemical facilities in Al-Ruwais in the UAE, which produce fuel material for US forces and military products for "Israel", resulting in widespread fire 4. US petrochemical facility in Sitra in Bahrain, which plays a role in producing petroleum derivatives needed by the US army. The attack resulted in a widespread fire and the destruction of important sections of this industry 5. US petrochemical facilities in Shuaiba, Kuwait, which led to a very extensive fire and the complete shutdown of this complex, which cooperated with the US armed forces The statement also warned that "what occurred today was only the first phase of a response that had been previously warned about," adding that any further attacks on Iranian civilian infrastructure would be met with "a multiplied response against the enemy’s interests anywhere in the region." As for the second phase of wave 96, it warned that it "will be carried out with more force and wider scope." "If you continue your acts of aggression and strike civilian facilities, our responses will be more forceful," the statement concluded.
